summary:
Prophylactic antibiotics are unnecessary for totally implantable venous access device placement because infection is mainly linked to suboptimal aseptic techniques and IV drug use—not procedural contamination. Guidelines support preventing infections through rigorous sterile procedures instead of antibiotic administration.

Second sentence must reflect what actual preventive strategies exist per current guidance.
